title: The Music of 3010
wikitext:
[]: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by intermediateO » Mon Jun 15, 2020 5:10 am Basically a speculation (and hopes) thread about the music of 3.0+1.0. There wasn't any noticeable theming for the music when 1.0 originally came out, but both 2.0 and 3.0 showed the pattern of Anno and Sagisu going back to their previous collaborations and reusing tracks. Looking back on 1.0, we could now say that maybe it just had some "stealth theming" by reusing NGE tracks. So... 1.0 - Neon Genisis Evangelion 2.0 - Kare Kano 3.0 - Nadia More interesting to me though, is that the works that the tracks came from seem to mirror some of the themes of the three films themselves too. 1.0 - Still basically paralleling the tv series, thus inconspicuous 2.0 - Introduces new romcom elements similar to Kare Kano 3.0 - Introduces Wille, which parallels the intentions and aesthetics of the crew of the Nautilus in Nadia So, I'm thinking that the final film will be... 3.0+1.0 - End of Evangelion (+Death and Rebirth maybe) End of Eva Speculation: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Jun 15, 2020 8:50 am Well at this point we can only wait and see, but: First of all, thank you for sharing the the Tweets, I wasn't aware of the fact the the guy who translated "Komm, süßer Tod" sings it, and that LOREN is involved! So cool! No mentioning of Arianne Schreiber, or Utada... yet...? intermediateO wrote: And personally, I think it is would be the perfect song for an inverse Komm, susser Tod sequence, the world turning blue as it bangs in the background.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Shun » Mon Jun 15, 2020 2:41 pm ELO - Twilight is a intriguing hypothesis. In line with the memories I would like if Shin Eva had the GunBuster's ending song, Toki no Kawa wo Koete, and the Pachelbel's Canon. And obviously a new song by Utada.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 15, 2020 3:22 pm robersora wrote: No mentioning of Arianne Schreiber, or Utada... yet...?

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Jun 15, 2020 3:59 pm ^ yeah, I remember there was something...

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by The_Max » Mon Jun 15, 2020 6:13 pm Talking about the music of 3.0+1.0, here you have the translation of the song Mari is singing in the opening, "Kiyoko Suizenji - Shinjitsu Ichiro no March": SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Shun » Tue Jun 16, 2020 6:55 am The song that Mari sings in Avant 1 is 真実一路のマーチ (Shinjitsu ichiro no māchi), "The march on the path of sincerity", by Kiyoko Suizenji. The song part that we hear in the preview is this: こ の 世 は 長 い 坂 道 だ け ど ~ 長 さ じ ゃ な い よ ~ 人生 は ~ 真 実 一路 ~ ~ 生 き た な ら 短 く た っ て か ま わ な い ~ ~ か ま わ な い Konoyo wa nagai sakamichidakedo 〜 naga sa janaiyo 〜 jinsei wa 〜 shinjitsuichiro 〜 ikitanara 〜 mijikaku tatte kamawanai 〜 kamawanai 〜 This world [of life] is a long climb, but it's not so much its length [the important thing], life is the path of sincerity, if I live it doesn't matter if it will be short, it doesn't matter Japanese lyrics: https://www.joysound.com/web/search/song/22989 真 実 一路 (Shinjitsuichiro) is a Japanese idiomatic expression consisting of four characters (yojijukugo) which means "path of sincerity". The meaning of this expression is to advance to seek the truth with a sincere and authentic heart, without hesitation and falsehood.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Jun 16, 2020 8:32 am I'm not crazy about Beautiful World (though I've become fond of it), but Sakura Nagashi I adore. I would have no issue with it coming back if it fits the film. What matters is it fitting.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Mr. M » Tue Jun 16, 2020 9:08 am Btw any news from Sagisu? Has he restarted working on the score?

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Jun 16, 2020 11:14 am Mr. M wrote: Btw any news from Sagisu? Has he restarted working on the score?

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Jun 16, 2020 11:23 am HAhahahaha that's amazing. The master has acknowledged the greatest transcended shitpost in existence

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 16, 2020 4:21 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: P.S. Random thought: I'm at work and can't check at the moment but I recently revisited Anno's Ritual (Shiki-Jitsu) for the first time in years (wonderful film) and I'm pretty sure the main character "She" sings top herself one of the songs that Mari sings to herself in the opening of one of the three Rebuild sequels. Regrettably I can't recall which one it was from or where in Ritual she sings to herself and am unable to check at the moment but might that be a callback too?

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Jun 16, 2020 4:54 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I know for a fact that Cutie Honey sings a song in Anno's live-action film adaptation that Mari sings in the beginning of 2.22.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by The_Max » Thu Jun 18, 2020 5:28 pm Shun wrote: The song that Mari sings in Avant 1 is 真実一路のマーチ (Shinjitsu ichiro no māchi), "The march on the path of sincerity", by Kiyoko Suizenji. The song part that we hear in the preview is this: こ の 世 は 長 い 坂 道 だ け ど ~ 長 さ じ ゃ な い よ ~ 人生 は ~ 真 実 一路 ~ ~ 生 き た な ら 短 く た っ て か ま わ な い ~ ~ か ま わ な い Konoyo wa nagai sakamichidakedo 〜 naga sa janaiyo 〜 jinsei wa 〜 shinjitsuichiro 〜 ikitanara 〜 mijikaku tatte kamawanai 〜 kamawanai 〜 This world [of life] is a long climb, but it's not so much its length [the important thing], life is the path of sincerity, if I live it doesn't matter if it will be short, it doesn't matter Japanese lyrics: https://www.joysound.com/web/search/song/22989 真 実 一路 (Shinjitsuichiro) is a Japanese idiomatic expression consisting of four characters (yojijukugo) which means "path of sincerity". The meaning of this expression is to advance to seek the truth with a sincere and authentic heart, without hesitation and falsehood.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Shun » Fri Jun 19, 2020 2:34 am @The_Max : My mistake, you're right! I had assumed that the second part was the continuum of the Kiyoko Suizenji song, while listening carefully is the Naomi Sagara song. Thanks for the info, I hadn't noticed this difference! So, there are two songs. 世界は二人のために Japanese lyrics: http://sp.utamap.com/showkasi.php?surl=36456

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by ErgoProxy » Sun Jun 21, 2020 7:12 pm 'K, may I please and have your attention now! BGM - B12 edition 0706 lyrics incoming: Fame is for famous Fate is for fated Now it's just published All that's outfiled[?] All that's not perished[?] All that's not buried[?] All that's for victory All that's forgotten Fate of fighting Fate of fighting for victory

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jun 21, 2020 7:48 pm I just noticed that if the movie hadn't been delayed we'd probably be mere weeks away from having the soundtrack. ;_;

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by intermediateO » Tue Dec 08, 2020 11:31 am So I'm so glad to see that Utada will be back to lend the theme to the final Eva movie, even though it took 14 years. I didn't take my Beautiful World one-last-reprise theory that seriously, even though it is what I actually would want to happen, it closing out all "3 parts" of the saga. With the announcements that the album would feature all of the previous music she provided before, I thought that fun theory was all but dashed. However, if you look closely at the Japanese track listing for the album, it ends with ほか, which means "other" or "and more". The title One Last Kiss also has a feel to it that surprisingly evokes the final epilogue of End of Eva, One More Final. So new theory fun: -Sakura Nagashi is still (symbolically) like the mid-credits song for the 3.0 saga. -Beautiful World will make a surprise mid-credits reprise in the film to finally close out the 3.0 saga. -The rest of the film will be the "+1.0" epilogue, with One Last Kiss playing as the background music to the very final scene.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by Mr. M » Tue Dec 08, 2020 1:51 pm Mmm... Sagisu in Avant1 used a slightly different version of "Persecution of the Masses" from Shin Godzilla, so it's not implausible a re use from it. The use of some tracks from EoE it's very likely, since all the hits Sagisu is putting. But could also implied that he just re-put the same team back, for another Finale. Why "spoil" the possible soundtrack like "Komm" if all of production it's still secret? Also, from teaser 3 Unit 08 and 02 were involved in a very much Gunbuster like scene, so I wouldn't be surprised if there's some tracks from it.

[]: Re: The Music of 3.0+1.0             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Dec 08, 2020 1:59 pm https://www.evangelion.co.jp/news/onelastkiss/ For whoever wants to pre-order it, here's the link! The pre-order link turns up right after the date in red. Yeah, it's interesting to think that the whole journey's spanned 13 years and Utada has been part of it throughout. And I can't begin to imagine how RxS shippers will react to this title!
